statement.p: Give an example of a cubic equation that has three solutions: one solution is x = 6\text{,} the second solution is x= -2\text{,} and the third solution is x = \frac{2}{3} \text{.} Write your equation in standard form.

solution.p: I will first write a cubic equation in factored form: (x-a)(x-b)(x-c) = 0 where a, b\text{,} and c are the solutions of the equation. Thus, we can write the cubic equation as (x-6)(x-(-2))(x - \frac{2}{3}) = 0 or (x-6)(x + 2)(x - \frac{2}{3}) = 0 \text{.}
